Spring, the luminous soul of life.
Nature opens before my eyes, filled with light,
and I follow its fragrance,
guided by the sun dancing over the meadows
and gently touching every creature.
I feel harmony breathing in the air,
a nature bursting into life:
the scent of the earth,
the quiet voice of the forests
where flowers bloom softly,
among fresh green leaves
painted in a thousand shades.
And in watching,
in simply gazing,
the soul awakens
and finally feels. Respect for you honored Prince Fazza ❤️ @faz3

أمريكا دولة الشر والاستكبار  تمدح ( دولة تعوسية آل سعود ...ودولية آل ناقص الإمارات اليهودية... ودويلة البحرين و قطر والكويت..)... وكل من تمدحه أمريكا فهو والله لقد خان الله ورسوله وخان دينه وأمته ما أبعدكم عن الله وكتاب الله ألم ينهاكم عن تولي اليهود والنصارى (  يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَتَوَلَّوۡاْ قَوۡمًا غَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيۡهِمۡ ...) ( ۞ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَتَّخِذُواْ ٱلۡيَهُودَ وَٱلنَّصَٰرَىٰٓ أَوۡلِيَآءَۘ بَعۡضُهُمۡ أَوۡلِيَآءُ بَعۡضٖۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم مِّنكُمۡ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنۡهُمۡۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهۡدِي ٱلۡقَوۡمَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ ) « فلعنة الله عليكم يا يهود الأمة يا من وضعت يدكم بيد ترامب قاتل آلاف المسلمين اطفال ونساء وشباب وكبار في غزة والعراق واليمن وإيران ولبنان »

Hello brothers and sisters, a Muslim brother needs a lung transplant surgery, please help if you have the opportunity.
For those who don't know, a lung transplant is a surgical procedure to replace a diseased or defective lung with a healthy lung from a deceased donor. The surgery is recommended for those who are in the final stages of lung disease, have limited life expectancy, and other treatments have failed. The surgery usually lasts 4-12 hours, depending on whether one or both lungs are being replaced.
The lung transplant surgery is in "India".
The cost is very expensive!!!
It ranges from $27,000 to $44,000.
Let's help this brother as much as we can.
This should be voluntary, you are not obligated, brother.
May Allah be pleased with us all.

The situation in the region is deteriorating rapidly. We are already receiving calls from Armenian families fleeing conflict in Iran and Lebanon, seeking safety in Armenia. Many are arriving with little to nothing.

At the Armenian Food Bank, we are actively preparing on the ground. Based on our experience responding to past refugee crises, we know one thing clearly: waiting is not an option. We must be ready before the wave arrives.

Our Immediate Priorities:

🔹 Emergency Food Supplies — ensuring no family goes hungry upon arrival

🔹 Rapid Response Logistics — preparing for large-scale arrivals
